claim_supported: Origin Quantum (spun out of the CAS Quantum Information key laboratory, Hefei) markets 'OriginQ Quantum AIO,' described as China's first domestically-developed integrated quantum-computer measurement-and-control system, supporting both superconducting and semiconductor qubits; the company also builds the Wukong-series superconducting quantum computers (72-qubit 'Wukong 180').
locator: Attempted direct fetch of a specific product-news subpage (本源天机4.0 announcement) returned only site navigation with no substantive content; claims above are sourced from WebSearch result snippets of originqc.com.cn and related pages, not independently fetched full text this session.
notes: Fetch attempt failed to retrieve substantive content (mobile redirect chain led to a near-empty page). Treat product-performance specifics (e.g. '本源天机4.0' channel counts) as unconfirmed until a verifier successfully fetches a content-bearing page.

title: QuEL, Inc. quantum control system deployed at RIKEN
authors_or_org: QuEL, Inc. (spun out of Osaka University)
claim_supported: March 27, 2023: RIKEN's quantum computer, unveiled that day, uses a control device developed at Osaka University and transferred to QuEL, Inc., which states the technology 'is already being sold' -- i.e. a Japanese national-lab deployment of a spun-out commercial control-hardware startup's product.
locator: quel-inc.com Japanese-language page, fetched in full this session
notes: Fetched successfully; company's own Japanese-language blog post, not an independent government confirmation, but names a specific national-lab (RIKEN) deployment.

title: Zynq UltraScale+ RFSoC Data Sheet (DS926 / DS889) -- RF-ADC/RF-DAC data converters used in quantum/accelerator control hardware
authors_or_org: AMD (Xilinx)
claim_supported: RFSoC RF-ADC supports up to 4 GSPS sampling / 4 GHz signal bandwidth; RF-DAC up to 6.554 GSPS / over 4 GHz output bandwidth, with built-in digital down/up converters, NCO, and complex mixer -- the underlying commodity silicon platform used by QICK (L13-001), QubiC (L13-025), and multiple commercial qubit-control vendors.
locator: docs.amd.com datasheet page (not independently fetched this session, found via WebSearch)
notes: Not fetched directly; official AMD documentation, high confidence despite not being fetched this session.

title: OPX+ / OPX1000 quantum control hardware
authors_or_org: Quantum Machines
claim_supported: Quantum Machines (Israel) markets OPX+ and OPX1000 as its current quantum-control hardware line; no public list pricing was found, though one secondary source estimates full entry-level cryogenic quantum-computing systems bundling either Qblox or QM control electronics cost roughly $1-3M plus $200-400K/year operations.
locator: quantum-machines.co product page (not independently fetched this session, found via WebSearch)
notes: The pricing figure is a third-party estimate (postquantum.com), not vendor-disclosed list pricing -- labeled as such.

title: ITRI develops 4K low-temperature control chip/module on TSMC 28nm process for quantum computing
authors_or_org: Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI, Taiwan); reported by EE Times Asia / EE Times Taiwan
claim_supported: ITRI's Electronics and Optoelectronics Systems Research Division built a 4K low-temperature control chip/module on TSMC's 28nm process, reducing quantum-computer control-instrument size by roughly 40%, integrated into the cooling system to shorten signal paths and reduce noise; verified interfacing with Academia Sinica's qubits.
locator: eettaiwan.com article (connection reset on WebFetch attempt this session; details per WebSearch summary)
notes: WebFetch attempt failed with a connection error this session; content summarized from WebSearch result only, not independently fetched. Complements the separate SEEQC-ITRI SFQ manufacturing partnership (L13-038), which is a distinct ITRI quantum-control-electronics initiative.

title: CAS press release on the Zuchongzhi 3.0 superconducting quantum-computing prototype
authors_or_org: Chinese Academy of Sciences (中国科学院)
claim_supported: Confirms the Zuchongzhi 3.0 results (L13-003) were published as a Physical Review Letters cover paper on March 3, 2025 (Beijing time); official CAS press statement, fetched in full.
locator: cas.cn official press release, fetched in full this session
notes: Fetched successfully; this page did NOT state a '100% domestic localization of cryogenic refrigeration and measurement-control system' claim seen in a separate, unfetched ncsti.gov.cn search snippet -- that specific localization claim should be treated as unconfirmed until independently fetched, despite appearing in multiple secondary Chinese news outlets.

title: USTC news office report on Zuchongzhi 3.0
authors_or_org: University of Science and Technology of China (中国科学技术大学) news office
claim_supported: Independently confirms Zuchongzhi 3.0's 105 qubits/182 couplers, 72 microsecond coherence, 99.90%/99.62%/99.13% parallel single-qubit/two-qubit/readout fidelities, and the 83-qubit/32-cycle random-circuit-sampling speed comparison versus classical supercomputers and Google's Willow processor; led by Pan Jianwei, Zhu Xiaobo, Peng Chengzhi.
locator: news.ustc.edu.cn official article, fetched in full this session
notes: Fetched successfully; a distinct institutional source (university, not the CAS umbrella) corroborating L13-003/048 performance figures; this article also did not address measurement-control-system localization specifics.

title: Dilution Refrigerator for Quantum Computing Market (industry market-size report)
authors_or_org: Valuates Reports / QYResearch (secondary market-research aggregator)
claim_supported: Estimates the dilution-refrigerator-for-quantum-computing market at roughly US$72.6M (current) growing to US$193M by 2031 (about 15% CAGR 2025-2031); broader all-application dilution-refrigerator market estimated at US$108M (2024) to US$178M (2032, 7.6% CAGR); Bluefors + Oxford Instruments together over 70% share (oligopolistic).
locator: valuates.com report landing page (not independently fetched this session, found via WebSearch)
notes: Consultancy/market-aggregator estimate with no disclosed bottom-up methodology -- T3, context only, not independently fetched. Should NOT be used alone as market-size evidence without independent triangulation per claim-discipline rules. Included as this lane's single T3 record (within the 2-3 T3 cap).

title: RRCAT Indus accelerator complex -- Accelerator Controls & Beam Diagnostics Division
authors_or_org: Raja Ramanna Centre for Advanced Technology (RRCAT), Department of Atomic Energy, Government of India
claim_supported: RRCAT's Accelerator Controls & Beam Diagnostics Division designs, develops, and maintains beam-diagnostic and control devices for India's Indus-1 (450 MeV) and Indus-2 (2.5 GeV) synchrotron radiation sources and their injector accelerators (20 MeV microtron plus booster synchrotron).
locator: rrcat.gov.in official division page (not independently fetched this session, found via WebSearch)
notes: Not fetched directly; a general facility-description page rather than a dated spending/procurement trigger -- flagged as thin evidence of a forward-looking 2026-2031 demand trigger specifically. India's synchrotron-control upgrade plans were not confirmed with a specific date/budget this session.

title: RD53 Collaboration -- pixel readout chips for ATLAS/CMS Phase-2 HL-LHC upgrades
authors_or_org: CERN (RD53 Collaboration), with Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory contributions
claim_supported: CERN's own EP-ESE page confirms the RD53 collaboration (established 2013) designs pixel-readout ICs for ATLAS/CMS Phase-2 HL-LHC upgrades under extreme radiation (1 Grad) and hit-rate (3.5 GHz/cm^2) requirements; Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory is separately confirmed (per WebSearch of physicalsciences.lbl.gov) as developing next-generation pixel-detector/readout-chip technology for the same program.
locator: ep-ese.web.cern.ch official CERN page (not independently fetched this session, found via WebSearch)
notes: Complements the JINST journal record L13-009 with the CERN/LBNL institutional/programmatic framing.
